items.title: Cerebras vs. SpaceX: Which Is the Better AI IPO Stock to Buy and Hold for the Next 10 Years?
items.summary: Cerebras and SpaceX are two major AI-linked IPOs with different business models. Cerebras specializes in wafer-scale AI processors for fast inference with strong OpenAI backing ($10B+ deal), while SpaceX leverages Starlink's recurring revenue base and expanding AI infrastructure business. SpaceX is recommended as the stronger 10-year pick due to multiple growth engines and lower dependency on single customers, though Cerebras offers higher upside potential if it executes on its ambitious roadmap.

items.title: Prediction: June 12 Will be a Huge Day for the Stock Market
items.summary: SpaceX is set to launch a major IPO on June 12, 2026, with 30% of shares allocated to retail investors. The company operates in AI, rocket launches, and satellite internet (Starlink). Goldman Sachs forecasts SpaceX's AI business could generate $322 billion in revenue by 2030, with total revenue reaching $474 billion. The IPO is expected to create market momentum that could benefit broader AI and technology stocks.

items.title: Oracle Joins Arm's AGI CPU Ecosystem As Agentic AI Boom Draws Meta, OpenAI And ByteDance
items.summary: Oracle has joined Arm Holdings' AGI CPU ecosystem, with CEO Rene Haas announcing that ByteDance is also now a customer. Arm's AGI CPUs, which offer more than twice the performance per rack of traditional x86 CPUs, are gaining significant traction among major tech companies including Meta, OpenAI, and Cerebras. The momentum is driven by the shift toward agentic AI systems, with Arm's energy-efficient architecture positioned to benefit from this industry evolution.

items.title: Why June 12 (or Soon After) Could Be a Make-or-Break Day for the Stock Market
items.summary: SpaceX is expected to go public on June 12, 2026, in what could be the largest IPO in history. The IPO's success or failure will serve as a critical indicator for the broader AI trade and stock market, as SpaceX is valued at $1.75-2 trillion with a 94-107x revenue multiple. The company is seeking unprecedented control for Elon Musk through supervoting shares and allocating 30% of shares to retail investors, making this a major test of market appetite for high-valuation AI and space economy companies.

items.title: Cerebras vs. Nvidia: Which AI Stock Is the Smarter Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Cerebras' impressive IPO has generated investor enthusiasm for its innovative wafer-scale AI chip technology. However, Nvidia remains the smarter investment choice due to its dominant 86% market share in AI data centers, $216 billion in annual revenue compared to Cerebras' $500 million, and proven profitability. While Cerebras shows promise with backing from OpenAI, it remains a risky bet compared to the established market leader.
